name: Ribera del Rio de las Yeguas — circular desde Casariche
name_original: Ribera del Río de las Yeguas — circular desde  Casariche
region_intro: Hiking around Casariche offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra Sur region of Seville. The area is characterized by the River Yeguas plain, extensive olive groves, and rugged terrain. Notable features include Cerro de La Atalaya, an archaeological site with Roman remains, and Cerro Bellido, known for its ancient Roman quarries and panoramic viewpoints. Best hiking trails around Casariche The most popular hiking route is Near the Genil River and Fuensanta Chapel — round trip from Badolatosa, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours to complete. This route explores the area around the Genil River and the historic Fuensanta Chapel. Another top favourite among local hikers is Arroyo del Pontón – Genil River Meander loop from Badolatosa, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.9 km) path. This trail follows the meanders of the Genil River, offering views of the river plain. Local hikers also love the Ribera del Rio de las Yeguas — circular desde Casariche, a 9.4 miles (15.2 km) trail leading through the River Yeguas plain, often completed in about 4 hours. Hiking around Casariche is defined by river plains, olive groves, and hills like Cerro de La Atalaya and Cerro Bellido. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate trails. The routes in Casariche are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Casariche's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Casariche, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around Casariche. The majority are suitable for most hikers, with 9 easy routes and 11 moderate routes. There are no difficult trails listed, making the area accessible for a wide range of abilities.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Casariche?
A: Hiking around Casariche offers a diverse landscape characterized by the &lt;strong&gt;River Yeguas plain&lt;/strong&gt;, extensive &lt;em&gt;olive groves&lt;/em&gt;, and the rugged terrain of the Sierra Sur region. You'll also encounter significant hills like &lt;strong&gt;Cerro de La Atalaya&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Cerro Bellido&lt;/strong&gt;, which provide both natural beauty and historical interest.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Casariche area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009919293/ribera-del-rio-de-las-yeguas-circular-desde-casariche" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera del Rio de las Yeguas — circular desde Casariche&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 9.4-mile trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250404247/gilena-from-estepa-circular-through-the-sierra-de-becerrero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gilena from Estepa — circular through the Sierra de Becerrero&lt;/a&gt;, offering a longer, moderate challenge.

Q: What interesting historical sites or natural attractions can I find along the trails?
A: The area is rich in history and natural beauty. You can visit the ancient &lt;em&gt;Roman quarries&lt;/em&gt; on Cerro Bellido or explore the archaeological site of &lt;strong&gt;Cerro de La Atalaya&lt;/strong&gt;, which features Roman remains. Natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Genil River Meander&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo del Pontón&lt;/a&gt;. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Fuensanta&lt;/a&gt;, often included in routes near the Genil River.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Casariche?
A: The trails in Casariche are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 out of 5 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from river plains to olive groves and hills, and the opportunities to discover historical sites and natural features.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Casariche offers 9 easy routes that are great for beginners or families looking for a less strenuous walk. While specific family-friendly designations aren't always given, these easier trails generally have gentler gradients and shorter distances, making them more accessible.

Q: Can I find trails that follow rivers or offer water views?
A: Absolutely. The region is defined by the &lt;strong&gt;River Yeguas plain&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Genil River&lt;/strong&gt;. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009928634/near-the-genil-river-and-fuensanta-chapel-round-trip-from-badolatosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Near the Genil River and Fuensanta Chapel — round trip from Badolatosa&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20118616" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo del Pontón – Genil River Meander loop from Badolatosa&lt;/a&gt; offer beautiful views of the Genil River and its meanders.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Casariche?
A: Given its location in Andalusia, the best times for hiking in Casariche are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities, and the landscape is often at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to viewpoints with panoramic vistas?
A: Yes, &lt;strong&gt;Cerro Bellido&lt;/strong&gt; is known for its viewpoint atop the hill, offering panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscape. Additionally, some routes may offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5394288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malpasillo Dam and Genil River&lt;/a&gt;, providing expansive scenic outlooks.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: While there are no trails classified as 'difficult,' experienced trekkers can enjoy longer moderate routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250404247/gilena-from-estepa-circular-through-the-sierra-de-becerrero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gilena from Estepa — circular through the Sierra de Becerrero&lt;/a&gt;, at over 10 miles, offers a good distance and elevation gain for a more extended outing. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009919293/ribera-del-rio-de-las-yeguas-circular-desde-casariche" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera del Rio de las Yeguas — circular desde Casariche&lt;/a&gt; is also a substantial moderate trail.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on the trails?
A: The terrain around Casariche is varied. You'll find relatively flat paths along the &lt;strong&gt;River Yeguas plain&lt;/strong&gt; and through &lt;em&gt;olive groves&lt;/em&gt;, as well as more undulating and rugged sections as you ascend hills like &lt;strong&gt;Cerro de La Atalaya&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Cerro Bellido&lt;/strong&gt;. Trails can range from dirt tracks to rocky paths, typical of the Sierra Sur region.
tips: Town that is at one end of the province of Seville and that is crossed by the Río de las Yeguas. The neuralgic center is located in the Plaza Maestro Vitito where the Parroquia Nuestra Señora de la Encarnación and the Kiosko de Música Maestro Vitito are located.

Road that is located between the towns that go from
Casariche to Puente Genil, following the Río de las Yeguas. The path that follows the riverside is surrounded by fields of olive trees, planted and offering a typical Andalusian landscape!
